integration.description: Conductor-owned canonical integration branch identity. fence-gate resolves ONLY this local branch or its origin-tracking twin (<remote>/<branch>) and measures each lane from the merge-base of the lane HEAD and that ref. No env override (FENCE_GATE_INTEGRATION_REF / FENCE_GATE_BASE) may substitute a different ref or base — most dangerously HEAD — and collapse the merge-base into a false pass.

sharedSurfaces.description: Only conductor-owned integration cards may change these after the fork; both lanes rebase and rerun their gates afterwards.

nonTargets.description: Byte-identical to main for the whole experiment.

frozenBehavior.description: Read-only lane inputs inside both proof games; hashes pinned in baseline/behavior-hashes.json and guarded by each game's frozen-behavior test. Every file under `dirs` is twin-identical and baseline-pinned EXCEPT the identity/laneOwned/declaredOutputs carve-outs below.

notes: A lane replacing its renderer (U6) edits ONLY its own game's src/main.ts, src/shell/TemplateShell.ts, src/shell/template-shell.css, and src/shell/renderers/** (plus renderer-only tests under tests/runtime/**), which the frozen-behavior test excludes from twin/baseline identity; src/core/**, src/sdk/**, src/game/**, src/shell/harness.ts, src/shell/insituTour.ts, content/**, and tests/unit/** stay frozen and twin-identical.

The audit `structure` linter now whitelists the exact top-level `authoring/` directory (card qWCv9tUo item 2), so a lane's editor-native authoring project is a legal top-level entry; near-miss names still fail.

Lane ownership is enforced by the executable fence verifier tools/verify-gate/fence-gate.mjs, measured from the trusted merge-base of the lane HEAD and the conductor-owned `integration.branch` above (or its origin twin), not from freeze.baselineCommit and not from any env-supplied ref/base — the seal commit necessarily follows the functional baseline and must not be attributed to any lane, and no override may collapse the base into HEAD.

Round-3 hardening (card qWCv9tUo comment 43): the policy that JUDGES a lane diff is loaded from the trusted BASE COMMIT (`git show base:fences.json`), never from the lane's working-tree bytes. The working-tree fences.json MUST byte-equal that canonical policy, so a lane cannot widen its own `writable` (e.g. to `**`), redirect `integration.branch` to its own branch (the base==HEAD collapse), or delete the policy to force a skip — all fail closed. A diverged branch with no FENCE_GATE_LANE also fails closed rather than silently skipping; only the integration tip (base==HEAD) or a conscious conductor FENCE_GATE_ALLOW_INTEGRATION acknowledgement skips.

The verifier reads changed paths NUL-safely from `git diff --raw -z -M -C --find-copies-harder` (name-status + modes): both the source and destination of a rename/copy are checked (`--find-copies-harder` exposes a pure copy from an UNCHANGED forbidden source that plain `-C` reports as a bare add), the deleted path is checked, and any changed tracked symlink (git mode 120000) is rejected even inside a writable glob.
